title: Add support for checking unsaved files, same as in XS/VS
body:
IFileSystem should be implemented, as was done in XS here: https://github.com/fsharp/fsharpbinding/pull/602

FSAC would then keep a list of modified file contents, and present those as fact to FCS via IFileSystem.
Editors are already sending modified file contents, so implementing this would improve error checking without editors needing to change anything.

title: Is the incorrect directory used for project references?
body:
I was testing FSharp.Atom on the FsharpBinding project and I noticed the following error:

```
Unable to find the file '/Users/dave/code/xamarin/monodevelop/main/external/fsharpbinding/MonoDevelop.FSharpBinding/bin/Debug/FSharpBinding.Gui.dll
```

In my fsprog the output path is set to

```
<OutputPath>..\bin\mac-linux\Debug\</OutputPath>`
```

Yet `bin/debug` is being used to find the reference, this seems really similar to this:

https://github.com/fsharp/FSharp.Compiler.Service/commit/2034e4e430ce6f3ca843cb115239628935b9a7e4

title: Completion command ignoring prefix text
body:
Let's say I've written `Sy` in editor and requested completion. From `FSharp.Autocomplete` I'm getting list of all possible completions (as nothing was written) instead of getting those completions which starts with (or contains) `Sy`. Is that by-design behavior? Or am I doing something wrong with it? Or we should try to change it?

Also maybe it would be nice to add Gitter room for this project? It could be good place to ask similar questions. (Or we can use F# slack channel but I'm not sure if all contributors / maintainers / people doing editor support are there, regularly I've been seeing  there @rojepp, @kjnilsson and @7sharp9 )

title: Do not include test assemblies in fsautocomplete.zip
FSharp.Autocomplete should use dirtyNotify callback to inform clients that files need parsing
body:
_From @rneatherway on April 17, 2014 9:11_

See #481. We now only parse buffers if they have changed, however something they depend on may have changed. FCS can inform fsautocomplete of this via the dirtyNotify callback. This can then be sent to a user of fsautocomplete (emacs) so that the editor knows to parse the buffer unconditionally.

title: fsautocomplete integration tests inconsistent because of helptext
body:
_From @rneatherway on November 25, 2014 18:7_

Because the helptext is returned synchronously, if it isn't ready then "(loading description...)" is returned. This cause problems on slow machines (like the CI environment). To work around this large waits have been inserted in the JSON tests (plain text doesn't support helptext), which works most of the time, but is ugly and unpredictable.

For testing we could instead use `DescriptionTextAsync` which 'Never returns "Loading...".' as mentioned here: http://fsharp.github.io/FSharp.Compiler.Service/reference/microsoft-fsharp-compiler-sourcecodeservices-fsharpdeclarationlistitem.html

Does not parse ProjectReferences?
body:
To reproduce:
1. Start a new project from the [F# Scaffold](https://github.com/fsprojects/ProjectScaffold)
2. Run `build.sh` a first time to generate the project
3. Open `tests/sample.Tests/Tests.fs` with Vim or Emacs, activate F# syntax checking

You'll see the editor complain that the `sample` module is undefined (`sample.Tests.fsproj` includes it as a `ProjectReference`). However, `build.sh` successfully compiles and runs the unit tests, so the project is set up correctly. I presume this indicates a bug in fsharpbinding?

Emacs complains about an undefined module/namespace. Vim gives me this:

`Unable to find the file '/tmp/ProjectScaffold-master/tests/sample.Tests/./bin/Debug/sample.dll' in any of /usr/lib/mono/4.0 /tmp/ProjectScaffold-master/tests/sample.Tests ...`

title: VS2015/F#4.0 installation not supported on Windows
body:
As reported by @xenocons at https://github.com/fsharp/fsharpbinding/issues/1019:

> To reproduce the issue,
> 
> Create a .fsx file somewhere else, and put the contents as:
> 
> ```
> #if INTERACTIVE
> #I @"C:\Users\user\Documents\Visual Studio 2013\Projects\test\";;
> #endif
> ```
> 
> Save this file, and close emacs, then load emacs again, then load this file, you will see fsautocomplete.exe crash with something like the following:
> 
> ```
> Unhandled Exception: System.Exception: Exception when getting check options for 'c:\Users\user\Desktop\test.fsx'
> .Details: System.IO.FileNotFoundException: Could not load file or assembly 'Microsoft.Build.Framework, Version=12.0.0.0, Culture=neutral, PublicKeyToken=b03f5f7f11d50a3a' or one of its dependencies. The system cannot find the file specified.
> File name: 'Microsoft.Build.Framework, Version=12.0.0.0, Culture=neutral, PublicKeyToken=b03f5f7f11d50a3a'
>    at Microsoft.FSharp.Control.AsyncBuilderImpl.commit[a](Result`1 res)
>    at Microsoft.FSharp.Control.CancellationTokenOps.RunSynchronously[a](CancellationToken token, FSharpAsync`1 computation, FSharpOption`1 timeout)
>    at Microsoft.FSharp.Control.FSharpAsync.RunSynchronously[T](FSharpAsync`1 computation, FSharpOption`1 timeout, FSharpOption`1 cancellationToken)
>    at FSharp.CompilerBinding.LanguageService.GetScriptCheckerOptions(String fileName, String projFilename, String source) in C:\Users\user\.emacs.d\fsharpbinding\FSharp.CompilerBinding\LanguageService.fs:line 299
> ```
> 
> Additionally, using fsautocomplete.exe and parsing the above file will cause the same exception.
> 
> I think it could perhaps be that the version 12 of the framework stuff is not in VS2015's version of F#? I tried this from a fresh elpa install of fsharp-mode.
> 
> Edit: Now that i think about it, this is probably due to CompilerServices not using .NET 4.6 yet?

title: Add command to return additional colorizations (new computation expressions keywords)
Automatically reparse projects if they are changed on disk
Output the tooltips is a more structured way, without formatting them up front
body:
Simplest would be to use the existing `FSharpToolTip` type and output an array of arrays, which would be an outer array of "things" with inner arrays of overloads (reverse patch):

```
-  type ToolTipElement =
-    {
-      Text: string
-      XmlDoc: Option<string>
-    }
-
   type OverloadParameter =
     {
       Name : string
@@ -293,28 +287,6 @@ module CommandResponse =
     member x.Declarations(decls) =
       x.WriteJson { Kind = "declarations"; Data = decls }

-    member x.ToolTipSymbol(FSharpToolTipText elements) =
-      let xmldoc cmt =
-        match cmt with
-        | FSharpXmlDoc.Text s -> Some s
-        | FSharpXmlDoc.None -> None
-        | FSharpXmlDoc.XmlDocFileSignature(file, key) -> Some (file + "," + key)
-
-      let data =
-        [ for e in elements do
-            match e with
-            | FSharpToolTipElement.None -> ()
-            | FSharpToolTipElement.CompositionError err ->
-                yield [ {Text = err; XmlDoc = None} ]
-            | FSharpToolTipElement.Single(it, comment) ->
-                yield [{ Text = it; XmlDoc = xmldoc comment }]
-            | FSharpToolTipElement.Group(items) ->
-                yield [ for it, comment in items do
-                          yield { Text = it; XmlDoc = xmldoc comment } ]
-          ]
-    
-      x.WriteJson { Kind = "tooltip"; Data = data }
-
     member x.ToolTip(tip) =
       x.WriteJson { Kind = "tooltip"; Data = TipFormatter.formatTip tip }
```

A more involved approach would be to use get the `FSharpSymbol` like is done in the XS support, and output information from that.

Some examples of current tooltips are:
- Normal: `List.map`

```
val map : mapping:('T -> 'U) -> list:'T list -> 'U list

Full name: Microsoft.FSharp.Collections.List.map
```
- Multiple items: type `Choice`

```
Multiple items
type Choice<'T1,'T2> =
  | Choice1Of2 of 'T1
  | Choice2Of2 of 'T2

Full name: Microsoft.FSharp.Core.Choice<_,_>

--------------------
```
- Multiple overloads: `Console.WriteLine`

```
Multiple overloads
Console.WriteLine() : unit
Console.WriteLine(value: bool) : unit
Console.WriteLine(value: char) : unit
Console.WriteLine(buffer: char []) : unit
```

title: Malformed json being produced when doc comment has quotes.
body:
I keep getting a malformed json error with the following code snippet when I put the cursor at SqlCommandProvider.  Once the error happens, fsharp-mode becomes useless as the status window is out of control.  Not sure if this is an FsAutoComplete issue or a Newtonsoft issue as to which is outputting the malformed json so posting here until I can dig deeper.

``` fsharp
module FSharpErrorTest

open System
open FSharp.Data
open FSharp.Data.SqlClient

[<Literal>]
let connectionString =
  @"Data Source=localhost;User Id=user; password=Test123;Initial Catalog=testdb;MultipleActiveResultSets=True"

type GetProject =
  SqlCommandProvider<"SELECT * FROM Projects WHERE Id = @id", connectionString>

let getProjectById id =
  use cmd = new SqlCommandProvider<"SELECT * FROM Projects WHERE Id=@id", connectionString>

  cmd.Execute(id)
  |> Seq.map (fun x -> x.Title)

title: Asynchronus FCS requests
body:
That's more question then true issue. I just wonder why most calls to FCS are run synchronously (only parse call is run async). It doesn't feel right (but I might be wrong as I'm only starting to understand what's going on here ;) ).

Another thing I'd like to ask (sorry for such multi-topic issue) is whether we could support cancellation of calls to FCS. I think it's reasonable to assume that if we get new request of given type on command queue client is not longer interested in past version just in newest request - we should cancel FCS call if current FCS call is for same request type and we should remove all requests of this type from command queue.

title: Integration with other tools using FCS
body: There exists other tools created by community such as [Fantomas](https://github.com/dungpa/fantomas) and [FSharpLint](https://github.com/fsprojects/FSharpLint) using FCS under the hood. It would be nice if we could integrate those tools into FSAC to not force editors to host multiple FCS sessions if they want use those tools. Also both tools provide API which can be used with AST, so they don't need to parse files again on their own.

title: StackOverflow exception
body:
I've started using Atom-fsharp developing one of my projects and I'm experiencing really a lot of StackOverflow exceptions - I've mentioned it some time ago but it was not as often as this time, so I've started investigated it bit more.

First observation (pointed out by smarter-than-me people from F# Slack channel): looks like our main function is not tail recursive - I was pointed out to this blog post http://vaskir.blogspot.com/2013/02/recursion-and-trywithfinally-blocks.html - which states that using `try ... with` in recursive function makes it not-tail recursive. 

Our main function has this nice `try ... with` which should work as global error handler but looks like we have here some side effect ;)

title: Handling colorization
body:
I was looking at possibility of simplifying Core API by making functions return result instead of list of results (discussed in #99). At the moment, there are 2 cases when we return multiple results - completion and helptext (https://github.com/fsharp/FsAutoComplete/blob/master/src/FsAutoComplete.Core/FsAutoComplete.Core.fs#L104-L105) and errors in case colorization is enabled (https://github.com/fsharp/FsAutoComplete/blob/master/src/FsAutoComplete.Core/FsAutoComplete.Core.fs#L21-L22). While we agreed to just remove helptext from completion result, I don't really know how to handle colorization case. 

Is colorization actually used in any editor at the moment?

title: 100% CPU usage - Reproducible (caused by complex dependency graph passed to project cracker)
body:
I originally logged this issue at the Ionide project https://github.com/ionide/ionide-vscode-fsharp/issues/55 but I think it needs to be here. This issue may in fact be with the compiler.cracker project but I'll leave that to the experts.

This issue looks like some type of circular dependency with project references. The graph is as follow:
1. Project A -> Project C
2. Project A -> Project B
3. Project B -> Project C

Loading the main.fs file in Project A with VS Code and Ionide pegs CPU at 100%. Removing any one of the project dependencies above from the relevant fsproj (A.fsproj or B.fsproj) file and the CPU remains normal. Limiting work to any .fs file(s) in Project B and/or Project C works fine with all project dependencies in place.

The following code will not produce a tooltip at the § location:

``` fsharp
let (|``BT§ST Immediate``|_|) data =

            let register = byte data &&& 0b111uy

title: `GetUsesOfSymbol` should narrow the scope it looks for symbol usage
body:
Here https://github.com/fsharp/FsAutoComplete/blob/master/src/FsAutoComplete.Core/CompilerServiceInterface.fs#L122 

In VFPT we classify a symbol into three categories: local for current file, local for current project, solution wide. After that we do the following:
- for file local symbol: `ParseAndCheckFileInProject` -> `GetUsesOfSymbolInFileAtLocation`
- for project local symbol: `ParseAndCheckProject` -> `GetUsesOfSymbol`
- for solution wide symbol: determine what projects the symbol is used in (see here https://github.com/fsprojects/VisualFSharpPowerTools/blob/master/src/FSharpVSPowerTools.Logic/ProjectFactory.fs#L211), then `ParseAndCheckProject` -> `GetUsesOfSymbol` for each of them.

title: FSAC cannot process valid fsproj files
body:
I believe this issue is due to the projectcracker

The file in question is [Ionide.FSharp.fsproj](https://github.com/ionide/ionide-vscode-fsharp/blob/master/src/Ionide.FSharp.fsproj)

when the fsharp targets path is resolved via 

``` xml
  <PropertyGroup Condition="Exists('$(MSBuildExtensionsPath32)\Microsoft\VisualStudio\v$(VisualStudioVersion)\FSharp\Microsoft.FSharp.Targets')">
    <FSharpTargetsPath>$(MSBuildExtensionsPath32)\Microsoft\VisualStudio\v$(VisualStudioVersion)\FSharp\Microsoft.FSharp.Targets</FSharpTargetsPath>
  </PropertyGroup>
  <Import Project="$(FSharpTargetsPath)" />
```

everything works fine, but change it to 

``` xml
  <Choose>
    <When Condition="$(TargetFSharpCoreVersion) >= 4.3.0.0 AND $(TargetFSharpCoreVersion) < 4.3.1.0 ">
      <PropertyGroup>
        <FSharpTargetsPath>$(MSBuildExtensionsPath32)\..\Microsoft SDKs\F#\3.0\Framework\v4.0\Microsoft.FSharp.Targets</FSharpTargetsPath>
      </PropertyGroup>
    </When>
    <When Condition="$(TargetFSharpCoreVersion) >= 4.3.1.0 AND $(TargetFSharpCoreVersion) < 4.4.0.0 ">
      <PropertyGroup>
        <FSharpTargetsPath>$(MSBuildExtensionsPath32)\..\Microsoft SDKs\F#\3.1\Framework\v4.0\Microsoft.FSharp.Targets</FSharpTargetsPath>
      </PropertyGroup>
    </When>
    <Otherwise>
      <PropertyGroup>
        <FSharpTargetsPath>$(MSBuildExtensionsPath32)\..\Microsoft SDKs\F#\4.0\Framework\v4.0\Microsoft.FSharp.Targets</FSharpTargetsPath>
      </PropertyGroup>    
    </Otherwise>
  </Choose>
  <Import Project="$(FSharpTargetsPath)" Condition="Exists('$(FSharpTargetsPath)')" />
```

it can no longer parse and resolve references properly. 
MSBuild can evaluate this choose branch properly in Visual Studio

**Related**  
- https://github.com/Microsoft/visualfsharp/issues/1435
- https://github.com/fsharp/FSharp.Compiler.Service/issues/624
- https://github.com/fsprojects/Fable/issues/346

title: Support msbuild version 15 fsproj format
body:
The .net core sdk preview3 (and soon the preview4) use msbuild 15

Some new features:

- `PackageReference` => first class support for nuget (from preview3)
- `Version` as attribute (from preview4)
- additional sdk from nuget packages, like F# from `FSharp.NET.Sdk`
- Conditionals in `PropertyGroup`/`ItemGroup`
- multiple target framework `TargetFrameworks`

the msbuild can also be run as `dotnet msbuild /t:MyTarget`

title: Intellisense doesn't work on double-tick discriminated union types
body:
Double-ticked variable names are pretty useful when you want to model complex domain.

However, autocomplete don't work on double-ticked discriminated union options. This makes using them quite painful.

Example:
```fsharp
type ``My Complex Type``=
| ``Pretty clear option 1 value here``
| ``Pretty clear option 2 value here``

let x1 = ``My Complex Type``.``Pretty clear option 1 value here``
let x2 = () // Just try to write
```

Tested on VS Code and Ionide.

title: Improve parsing of fproj project (new sdk)
body:
Current implementation for new sdk (the .net core sdk), starts reading from an fsproj project file and from there traverse the project references to parse and discover more projects.

From each project, to get fsc args and project references, msbuild is called as process.

Some possibile improvements (from easier to harder):

- cache already parsed projects (if A ref B, and C ref B, than B should be parsed just once). ref https://github.com/fsharp/FsAutoComplete/blob/9f70f0b299ec6a7626608c0a3612e10844c93ddf/src/FsAutoComplete.Core/ProjectCoreCracker.fs#L93 . Helps if deep dependency tree, with common projects
- Run in parallel the msbuild processes. Helps if broad dependency tree, if a project is flat with multiple project references
- Invoke msbuild process async, no need to wait
- Lock msbuild executions, because async request may run msbuild on same project concurrently (one will wait and fail, but waste resources)
- Use a small pool of msbuild processes

all changes should be inside https://github.com/fsharp/FsAutoComplete/blob/master/src/FsAutoComplete.Core/ProjectCoreCracker.fs

title: Try infer current target framework, if multiple target frameworks (crossgen)
body:
If fsproj use 

```xml
 <TargetFrameworks>netstandard1.6;net40</TargetFrameworks>
```

can build both target frameworks.

But because fsc args (used to initialize FCS) are framework specific, we need to choose one, if the FSAC client doesnt choose a preference.
Is alike of preference about `Debug` for `Configuration`

I think using the first `netstandard*` or `netcoreapp*` will be ok.

title: Support new new sdk (RFC FS-1032)
body:
The `FSharp.NET.Sdk` is now deprecated in favor of `Microsoft.NET.Sdk`, ref https://github.com/fsharp/fslang-design/blob/master/RFCs/FS-1032-fsharp-in-dotnet-sdk.md

How to test it:

- download a dev build of [dotnet/cli in branch release/2.0.0](https://github.com/dotnet/cli/tree/release/2.0.0). tested with `2.0.0-preview2-006391`
- usual `dotnet new console -lang f#` etc..

There is little difference from FSAC side, target files are pretty much the same, same msbuild based:

- [X] how to detected new sdk (vs old project cracker based): 
    ```xml
    <Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk">
    ```
    instead of 
    ```xml
    <Project Sdk="FSharp.NET.Sdk;Microsoft.NET.Sdk">
    ```
    => is already ok with https://github.com/fsharp/FsAutoComplete/commit/13a2f30eea9321b1586981490841b126a6c1e555


- [x] fsc args, file path: the old implementation used always full path for files. Now path of files passed to fsc are relative to fsproj directory. (wip in #166)


Instead of adding a target file inside `obj` dir, is possibile to use `FSharpDesignTimeTargetsPath` property to pass the target file to import (more clean, work the same, so just an enhancement). Do this is is detected the sdk as point 1 => added https://github.com/enricosada/dotnet-proj-info/issues/4 to track that


/cc @Krzysztof-Cieslak @rneatherway @dsyme

title: MsBuild madness
body:
Looks like it's windows only issue, mono as always works better. 

So at the moment, FSAC doesn't work if MsBuild 14 is not installed - what's kinda annoying for users . Main 2 problems are:
* if someone has just VS 2017 installed [that installs MsBuild 15]  they need to install MsBuild 14 additionally; 
* MsBuild 15 is also latest version available using `chocho` so it forces users to provide specific version... and not everyone reads readme and it's constant source of problems ;)

The question is (and I guess I should know answer... ) why we even depend on specific MsBuild version?
My best bet would be that it's explicit ProjectCracker dependency - https://github.com/fsharp/FSharp.Compiler.Service/blob/master/src/fsharp/FSharp.Compiler.Service.ProjectCrackerTool/ProjectCrackerTool.fs#L127-L129

Couple of questions:
Will ProjectCracker work with MsBuild 15? Fix should be as simple as fixing this MsBuild lookup I've linked above
Or maybe @enricosada's tool for cracking new fsprojs could also work with old fsprojs? In my experience it's working better than ProjectCracker

CC: @dsyme

title: add a `workspace-peek` command
body:
Scenario:

An editor open a directory (usually), and now need to know what's interesting inside, and that mean fsproj and sln files, to start.

This is a lot more important in new `dotnet/sdk`, because:

- restore. if not restored, a `dotnet restore` command should be run. ideally from a sln instead of multiple times. but if a root project exists, is ok too. But is not easy to know, but user does.
- mixed fsproj of old sdk and new sdk in same repo. happen a lot atm, to just support netcore/netstandard. usuallly they are in different sln, but not always.
- sln are not always used
- for initialization with `project`, the starting project matter a lot. really important with multiple framework

So an editor need to choose where to start, and FSAC can help.

## Proposed

a new `workspace-peek` command who accept a directory, and return info about all sln/fsproj **fast but inaccurate**. 
i want to emphatize **fast and inaccurate**, because my idea is just a quick peek, to better understand how continue. Implementation will do just xml parsing/grep, not real msbuild automation.
as usual `project` will do the detailed parsing and initialization after.

Info returned for each fsproj:

- lib or exe?
- sdk type? `old sdk` vs `project.json`  vs `dotnet/sdk` vs `fsharp.net.sdk`.
- target frameworks? one or more?
- graph of p2p or topological sort or weight of importance (useful for picker to sort choices for user)
- tags, like `test`, `fable`, `fez` (soon™️), `paket` etc

for sln, just proj list is enough

@Krzysztof-Cieslak @rneatherway @kjnilsson feedback?
this is easier to move code from editor side to FSAC, and implement easier nice UX sharing code who already exists there anyway (what is the sdk type? etc).

Possibile alternatives:

- a more accurate `workspace` but slower initialization step, with real msbuild automation, to replace `project`, but need some feedback about request/response, because some choices should be left to user. So maybe later

title: Persistent caching of project data
body:
One of the problem we have right now in Ionide is slow initialization process - what is caused by rather slow process of cracking projects. It's especially visible on the bigger solutions (20,30,40 or more projects). 

I think one of the way to make this better and faster would be introducing persistent caching for the parsed project data - we could just save them to disk and use on next start if project file hasn't change. I believe it would create much better UX for most users in most cases

title: Build fails due to missing VisualStudioVersion
body:
This is more of a question (so far) than an issue report: I know why the build fails, but I'm not sure whether it was made so intentionally.

* The `fsproj` file [depends on the `VisualStudioVersion` variable](https://github.com/fsharp/FsAutoComplete/blob/master/src/FsAutoComplete/FsAutoComplete.fsproj#L42) to import `Microsoft.FSharp.targets` from the right location, but that variable is not defined anywhere.
* Normally, this variable would be defined in `Microsoft.Common.props`, but the project doesn't import that either. 
* As a result, the project tries to import file `(...)\VisualStudio\v\FSharp\Microsoft.FSharp.Targets`, which does not exist.
* In addition, the project explicitly ignores this situation by specifying `Condition="Exists('$(FSharpTargetsPath)')"` on the `<Import>` tag, which means that the error message I get is not "_FSharp.targets not found_", but rather "_The project does not have a Build target_". So it took some digging to figure out what was going on.

So the questions are:
* Was `Microsoft.Common.props` import removed on purpose or was it a mistake?
* If on purpose, should a default fall-back definition of `VisualStudioVersion` be added instead?
* Was the condition added to the `<Import>` tag on purpose? If so, why? (this seems totally nonsensical: if the import is not there, why bother continuing the build?)

title: watch for `project.assets.json` changes
add a `workspace-load` command
body:
Atm FSAC works as project based, but that constrain a lot the initialization, and the logic must be inside clients (vim/emacs/vscode).
That logic can be complex, but usually scenario are just few ( project/script/sln/directory ).
Also the logic can include some workflow with interaction with user/config/default , to allow better UX

With #181 (`workspace-peek`) FSAC now has lot more info about the workspace (solution or directory based).

Proposed is a `workspace-load` who accept a:
- [ ] `solution`
- [x] `directory`  (pratically like now), so a list of fsprojs
- [ ] `fsx` from a script file

and will start initialization of all projects in that, in background.

some initialization options can be:

- [ ] `Configuration` (`Debug`/`Release`)
- [ ] target framework.

The workspace can be new entity, first class.
this allow:
- [ ] unload
- [ ] watch for changes to projects (to automatically reload changed proj, etc)

~Blocked~ by missing push events https://github.com/fsharp/FsAutoComplete/issues/193

title: Possibly incorrect `isOperator` in Lexer.fs (or the code that uses it in cases with DOT)
body:
Case:

```fsharp
    type T1 =
      static member P1 = ""
    T1.P
```

Let's see how Lexer `getSymbol` works for the last line. It calls
`tokenizeLine` which gives 3 tokens. The second token representing `.` is:

        CharClass   Delimiter   Microsoft.FSharp.Compiler.SourceCodeServices.FSharpTokenCharKind
        ColorClass  Punctuation Microsoft.FSharp.Compiler.SourceCodeServices.FSharpTokenColorKind
        FSharpTokenTriggerClass MemberSelect    Microsoft.FSharp.Compiler.SourceCodeServices.FSharpTokenTriggerClass
        FullMatchedLength   1   int
        LeftColumn  2   int
        RightColumn 2   int
        Tag 77  int
        TokenName   "DOT"   string

Note the color class is `Punctuation`, not `Operator`.

Here is the definition of `isOperator`

https://github.com/fsharp/FsAutoComplete/blob/786326ee6420e2d6b07fd8d9c5b4899916294341/src/FsAutoComplete.Core/Lexer.fs#L50

Thus, it is not supposed to treat the dot in our case as operator.

As a result, when `getSymbolFromTokens` calls `fixTokens` in order to convert
the original tokens to `DraftToken`, the dot becomes `{Kind = Other; ...}`,
not `{Kind = Operator; ...}`.

But the code in Lexer looks like the dot is expected to be `{Kind = Operator; ...}`.

Examples (note `Kind = Operator` and `Tag = FSharpTokenTag.DOT`):

https://github.com/fsharp/FsAutoComplete/blob/786326ee6420e2d6b07fd8d9c5b4899916294341/src/FsAutoComplete.Core/Lexer.fs#L135-L136

https://github.com/fsharp/FsAutoComplete/blob/786326ee6420e2d6b07fd8d9c5b4899916294341/src/FsAutoComplete.Core/Lexer.fs#L147

So it looks like either `isOperator` is not correct or the code that assumes DOT is `Operator`.

title: Unify suave and stdio console app
body:
Currently there is `fsautocomplete.exe` and `FSAutocomplete.Suave.exe`, but are pretty much the same.

Proposed is to unify `FSAutocomplete.Suave` inside `fsautocomplete.exe`, adding a `--transport http` to start fsac. default continue to be stdio

Differences atm are minimal, upgrade is easy (who use suave just change the name of exe, and pass the new argument)

Pro: 
- maintenance
- will help soon when we move to a add `netcoreapp2.0` (.net core) version of fsautocomplete
- unifying the output format, can help run same test suite output for both stdio and http

### json serializer: options

stdio and suave output is pretty similar.

The only difference atm is the `OptionConverter` ( [src/FsAutoComplete.Suave/JsonSerializerSuave.fs#L12](https://github.com/fsharp/FsAutoComplete/blob/master/src/FsAutoComplete.Suave/JsonSerializerSuave.fs#L12)  who convert an `'T option` so use null, instead of a more verbose value

```json
      "MyOptionalValue": {
        "Case": "Some",
        "Fields": [
          {
              //here the option type
          }
        ]
      }
     }
```

to just (if `Some`)

```
      "MyOptionalValue": {
              //here the option type
     }
```

or if `None`

```json
      "MyOptionalValue": null
```


Checking the code, in the API of commands, is used only in the linter (and in new additional info of `project` response, but that's not yet used by anyway)

Proposed: use `Suave` convention

### command line parser

Suave use `Argu`, stdio use `NDesk.Options.OptionSet`, but that's just for command line args, so is easy to migrate. 

Proposed: use `Argu`


/cc @rneatherway @Krzysztof-Cieslak @kjnilsson @nosami

title: client should choose what dotnet and msbuild to use
body:
now for parsing of fsproj is used the `dotnet`, so resolve that in PATH or from global.json.

That mean the dotnet used, is somewhat fixed based on environment of client.

An example: opening vscode from a terminal, will use the `dotnet` from PATH, and maybe is not the right one for that repo

Also `msbuild` should be found in the usual install locations, and defaulted to that if not found in PATH

title: Can't build on Linux
body:
```
$ ./build.sh release
Checking Paket version (downloading latest stable)...
Copying version 5.96.1 from cache.
Done.
Cannot open assembly '.paket/paket.exe': File does not contain a valid CIL image.
```

Have you experienced this @enricosada ?

I am using

    Mono JIT compiler version 5.2.0.215 (tarball Mon Aug 14 15:46:23 UTC 2017)

title: project Output path is wrong for dotnet/sdk project
body: the `Output` path of dotnet/sdk project is the `obj` assembly instead of the `bin` assembly

title: Very slow and CPU/RAM usage intensive with big dotnetcore project
body:
I use Ionide (3.1) to load FAKE 5 new solution (with 32 smalls dotnet core projects, each with 2 or 3 frameworks). Ionide plugin seems to be blocked on "Loading all projects"...looking at Task Manager, I got a high CPU/RAM usage by fsautocomplete and some  dotnet command line (launched by fsautocomplete?).

I imagine that having so many projects does not help, and many target frameworks neither...do you see any workaround to make it usable?

Thanks

title: support cancel request on changes
body:
in VS reactor requests are cancelled aggressively as you type,
FSAC does not (editors should notify it), so you will get 40 seconds pauses to get tooltips and completion instead of 20 seconds ones

title: fsi variable is unavailable when parsing script files
body:
With FSAC 0.34.0 and F# 4.1.18 on Linux:

```
arch-vm[1353]:~% mono ~/.emacs.d/elpa/fsharp-mode-20170918.842/bin/fsautocomplete.exe -v
{"Kind":"info","Data":"Started (PID=109693)"}
parse "FsiBug.fsx"
do printfn "%A" fsi
<<EOF>>
{"Kind":"info","Data":"Background parsing started"}
{"Kind":"errors","Data":{"File":"/home/mdl/FsiBug.fsx","Errors":[{"FileName":"/home/mdl/FsiBug.fsx","StartLine":1,"EndLine":1,"StartColumn":17,"EndColumn":20,"Severity":"Error","Message":"The value or constructor 'fsi' is not defined. Maybe you want one of the following:\n   fst","Subcategory":"typecheck"}]}}
parse "FsiBug2.fsx"
#r @"/usr/lib/mono/fsharp/FSharp.Compiler.Interactive.Settings.dll"
do printfn "%A" fsi
<<EOF>>
{"Kind":"info","Data":"Background parsing started"}
{"Kind":"errors","Data":{"File":"/home/mdl/FsiBug2.fsx","Errors":[]}}
```

Notice that it works if you explicitly reference `FSharp.Compiler.Interactive.Settings.dll`--but you should not have to do this. With the same version of FSAC running on the CLR on Windows, `FsiBug.fsx` parses fine, which is the correct/expected behavior.

Mono version info, just in case it's useful:

```
arch-vm[1002]:~% mono --version
Mono JIT compiler version 5.7.0 (master/5825342717c Mon Oct  2 17:12:43 JST 2017)
Copyright (C) 2002-2014 Novell, Inc, Xamarin Inc and Contributors. www.mono-project.com
        TLS:           __thread
        SIGSEGV:       normal
        Notifications: epoll
        Architecture:  amd64
        Disabled:      none
        Misc:          softdebug
        LLVM:          supported, not enabled.
        GC:            sgen (concurrent by default)
```

title: Crash when missing file reference in .fsproj
body:
The following project file will cause FsAutoComplete to crash:

```
<Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk">

  <PropertyGroup>
    <OutputType>Exe</OutputType>
    <TargetFramework>netcoreapp2.0</TargetFramework>
  </PropertyGroup>

  <ItemGroup>
    <Compile Include="MyModule.fs" />
    <Compile Include="MyMissingModule.fs" />
    <Compile Include="Program.fs" />
  </ItemGroup>

</Project>

```

The symptom in Visual Studio Code is the following message in the Output Window for the F# Language Service:
```

[13:57:56 INFO ] Logging to output at level INFO. If you want detailed messages, try level DEBUG.
[13:58:05 ERROR] RES (003) <- {parse} ERROR in 1018 ms: {stack: Error: connect ECONNREFUSED 127.0.0.1:8842
	at Object.exports._errnoException (util.js:1050:11)
	at exports._exceptionWithHostPort (util.js:1073:20)
	at TCPConnectWrap.afterConnect [as oncomplete] (net.js:1097:14), message: connect ECONNREFUSED 127.0.0.1:8842, code: ECONNREFUSED, errno: ECONNREFUSED, syscall: connect, address: 127.0.0.1, port: 8842, config: [object Object], request: [object Object], response: undefined} Data=undefined

```
There is no warning about the real error (a missing file). It just crashes.

FsAutoComplete should fail gracefully in this case and at least log a meaningful error.




[testproject.zip](https://github.com/fsharp/FsAutoComplete/files/1403311/testproject.zip)

title: 32bit version of fsiautocomplete.exe
body:
Refering to this issue https://github.com/ionide/ionide-vscode-fsharp/issues/642# there is a issue to get intellisense working when you're using the 32bit version of the FSharp.Data TypeProvider. For details why please have a look at this issue.
To fix it it's required to run FsAutoComplete as 32 bit. There seems to be no good solution to run the AnyCPU version on a 64 bit OS as 32 bit. Maybe we can compile theFsAutoComplete.exe as 32 bit just as the fsi.exe?

title: add `--mode lsp` (language server protocol)
body:
add another mode `lsp`, to implement [Language Server Protocol](https://www.github.com/Microsoft/language-server-protocol)

while not useful to current editors with better support (like vim/emacs/ionide), will help others less maintained like

- [Atom Editor](https://atom.io/)
- [Sublime Text](https://www.sublimetext.com/)
- [Eclipse Che](https://github.com/eclipse/che/)

More info in https://langserver.org/

title: websocket notification split the message
body:
Dont know if is an issue with websocket client or server side (in suave).

Know facts:

- happen both using `System.Net.WebSocket` and [js websockets/ws](https://github.com/websockets/ws) as client, so probably is server side.
- happen at random, not just for big message or for lot of small messages.

An example is https://ci.appveyor.com/project/rneatherway/fsautocomplete/build/1.0.808/job/u44oie2k0dkvfk69#L1233 where the single json message is splitted in two (see the `+`) and so not normalized (test suite normalize the output single line, not multiline).

in the test suite, i tried to workaround [incrementing the websocket client buffer](https://github.com/fsharp/FsAutoComplete/blob/1dec136baa71aa63d8b80cb242fba1afa631cede/test/FsAutoComplete.IntegrationTests/TestHelpers.fsx#L191), but doesnt work always. removing that happen more often

code of websocket server is https://github.com/fsharp/FsAutoComplete/blob/263c4ccdccf421983ca8f6b2795187370b0cbe52/src/FsAutoComplete/FsAutoComplete.Suave.fs#L67-L117

title: IntelliSense doesn't work for Office interop assemblies
body:
ref  https://github.com/ionide/ionide-vscode-fsharp/issues/714

Consider the following code script:

```fsharp
#if INTERACTIVE
#r "Microsoft.Office.Interop.Outlook.dll"
#endif
open Microsoft.Office.Interop.Outlook

let getFullName userName =
    let outlook = ApplicationClass()
    let message = outlook.CreateItem(OlItemType.olMailItem) :?> MailItem
    message.Recipients.Add(userName) |> ignore
    message.Recipients.ResolveAll() |> ignore
    message.Recipients
    |> Seq.cast<Recipient>
    |> Seq.map (fun r -> r.AddressEntry.GetExchangeUser().Name)
    |> Seq.head
```

The project loading fails to resolve `Microsoft.Office.Interop.Outlook` 

checked in vs2015 and load correctly.

It doesnt work with neither new project loader nor projectcracker

title: completion with backticks doesnt work
body:
Completion with backticks doesnt send correct value as text to insert

ref https://github.com/ionide/ionide-vscode-fsharp/issues/726

```fsharp
open System

module CommonPaketFiles =
    let  ``opennlp.sourceforge.net`` = ()

let f () =
    CommonPaketFiles.|
```

With completion at `|` the result shold be

```fsharp
let f () =
    CommonPaketFiles.``opennlp.sourceforge.net``
```

instead is

```fsharp
let f () =
    CommonPaketFiles.opennlp.sourceforge.net
```

title: Combine .NET Framework and .NET Core fsproj and sln files
body:
With VS 15.6, multi-targeting projects appear to be working properly in the IDE.

I suggest that we combine the project files if possible.

I ran into errors when trying to benchmark some tests on .NET Core where unrelated fsproj for .NET framework was throwing errors despite not even being used. After deleting the .NET framework fsproj, the tests ran properly.
`The value "" of the "Project" attribute in element <Import> is invalid. Parameter "path" cannot have zero length.
`
I am hoping that combining will make a lot of things easier like benchmarking under different build targets.

title: StackOverflow when providing Namespace Suggestions
body:
I ran into an FSAC stackoverflow while editing today that seems to be related to the `GetNamespaceSuggestions` command.

It happens consistently on my repository, and the stack trace is truly huge. It's roughly 30k lines.
The interesting bit happens towards the very bottom: 

```
  at Microsoft.FSharp.Core.CompilerServices.RuntimeHelpers.takeInner@267<T_REF, TResult_REF> (Microsoft.FSharp.Core.CompilerServices.RuntimeHelpers/ConcatEnumerator`2<T_REF, TResult_REF>,Microsoft.FSharp.Core.Unit) [0x00022] in <5a7d678a904cf4daa74503838a677d5a>:0
  at Microsoft.FSharp.Core.CompilerServices.RuntimeHelpers/ConcatEnumerator`2<T_REF, TResult_REF>.System-Collections-IEnumerator-MoveNext () [0x0001f] in <5a7d678a904cf4daa74503838a677d5a>:0
  at Microsoft.FSharp.Collections.SeqModule.ToList<T_REF> (System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable`1<T_REF>) [0x0003f] in <5a7d678a904cf4daa74503838a677d5a>:0
  at <StartupCode$FsAutoComplete-Core>.$Commands/GetNamespaceSuggestions@495.Invoke (Microsoft.FSharp.Core.Unit) [0x0013a] in <5afaae6db98589b7a74503836daefa5a>:0
```
So the GetNamespaceSuggestions command ends up calling ToList on a Seq and that Seq goes very, very, very deep. The majority of the ~30k lines are Seq combinators.  It appears there's some kind of infinite nonrecursive loop?

This is on Ionide 3.20.4 on mono 5.12.

[StackTrace-StackOverflow.txt](https://github.com/fsharp/FsAutoComplete/files/2018448/StackTrace-StackOverflow.txt)

title: XML docs produced by C# compiler are interpreted as markdown code blocks
body:
To reproduce this, create a C# class library and reference it from an F# project, note the formatting of the XML docs:

``` csharp
namespace cslib {
    public class Class1 {
        /// <summary>
        /// Does something.
        /// </summary>
        public void DoSomething() { }
    }
}
```

The generated documentation file looks like this:

``` xml
<?xml version="1.0"?>
<doc>
    <assembly>
        <name>cslib</name>
    </assembly>
    <members>
        <member name="M:cslib.Class1.DoSomething">
            <summary>
            Does something.
            </summary>
        </member>
    </members>
</doc>

```

The leading whitespace in the XML document causes the text to be interpreted as a code block in VS Code:

![Screenshot - Summary rendered as code block](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/11393003/43388575-d31a760c-93e9-11e8-9079-cd5df2e47a59.png)

 There is [a small utility](https://github.com/bartsokol/XmlDoc2Md) which converts XML documentation files to Markdown documents. Maybe we can reuse [some of the code](https://github.com/bartsokol/XmlDoc2Md/blob/23229efc42b9ef2321519b64b63db82d661aa5dd/Program.fs#L116-L131)?

title: Potential issue resolving FSharp.Core to use for Script Files
body: Copy/pasting slack thread here: ``` Fabio Marreco [09:50] Hey guys, sorry for the lame question... I´ve just update VS to 15.8, and after that ionide went crazy. After most of the errors, I´m stuck with this: (I´m guessing the language server is pointing to a different F# compiler version, but i dunno where to correct this) chethusk [11:34] @Fabio Marreco you're having ionide woes? can you try something for me: you chould have fsianycpu.exe in a folder like `c:\Program Files (x86)\Microsoft Visual Studio\2017\Community\Common7\IDE\CommonExtensions\Microsoft\FSharp\`. do you have that path? if so, you can try setting the VSCode config option `FSharp.fsiFilePath` to the full path to `fsianycpu.exe` in that folder, restarting vscode, and seeing if that changes things Fabio Marreco [12:19] @chethusk :white_frowning_face: no good. Same thing. I Thought this setting (`FSharp.fsiFilePath`) was only for the REPL (which works), no ? chethusk [12:21] Yeah, it was a bit of a stab in the dark. Can you submit an issue to ionide? It's probably a problem in FSAC underneath us that we have to track down Fabio Marreco [12:25] Will do. Is there any log that might help ?(for instance, a log stating which version of F# it is using) chethusk [12:32] Yes, the ionide repo should have instructions on it for getting the extension logs if I remember correctly. That would help us see what FSAC thinks the dependencies of your script are Ooh, thought: @cartermp is there an Fsharp.core colocated with fsi anymore? (edited) cartermp [12:34] Nope Well, I suppose there could be But we use the package for everything chethusk [12:36] Yesssss, good. That's my sneaking suspicion, that we no longer find an fsharp.core chethusk [12:42] @Fabio Marreco do you have an `FSharp.Core.dll` in any of the following locations? • C:\Program Files (x86)\Reference Assemblies\Microsoft\FSharp\.NETFramework\v4.0\4.4.1.0 • C:\Program Files (x86)\Reference Assemblies\Microsoft\FSharp\.NETFramework\v4.0\4.4.0.0 • C:\Program Files (x86)\Reference Assemblies\Microsoft\FSharp\.NETFramework\v4.0\4.3.1.0 • C:\Program Files (x86)\Reference Assemblies\Microsoft\FSharp\.NETFramework\v4.0\4.3.0.0 Fabio Marreco [12:47] 4.3.00 4.3.0.0, 4.3.1.0 and 4.4.0.0 all have and they are older versions of FSharp.Core.dll chethusk [12:49] but no 4.4.1.0? Fabio Marreco [12:50] no 4.4.1.0 chethusk [12:50] cool, thanks ok, last question @Fabio Marreco. what `FSharp.fsacRuntime` are you using? (if you don't know, you're on `net`) (edited) Fabio Marreco [12:54] i´ve tried "net" and "netcore", both give the same error. chethusk [12:54] oh that's interesting ok, well in the short term, you should be able to fix your script issues by manually adding a reference to `FSharp.Core.dll` from a path on your system. the easiest way to do this is probably to use nuget/paket to install it to your nuget cache and then reference it from `~/.nuget/packages/fsharp.core//lib//FSharp.Core.dll`, where `` is going to depend on which nuget version you want (probably 4.5.2), and `` is going to be `net45` probably brb embedding paket into FSAC so that we can download nuget packages (edited) Fabio Marreco [13:09] thanks. you mean reference by using #r "........ .dll" ? If so, same result. Thought it is not critical for me, I can always use VS meanwhile. But I do prefer ionide for smaller projects / scripts I guess VS decided to do something different when it installed on my machine :thinking_face: Fabio Marreco [13:25] 1) I guess the `#r` did not work for the same reason it doesn´t work on FSI: *error FS1222: When mscorlib.dll or FSharp.Core.dll is explicitly referenced the --noframework option must also be passed* chethusk [13:25] we do some extra scrubbing in FSAC to pass that flag for you if you explicit pass a reference to FSharp.Core :smile: (edited) Fabio Marreco [13:25] 2) I´ve simply renamed `C:\Program Files (x86)\Reference Assemblies\Microsoft\FSharp\.NETFramework\v4.0` to `XXXXv4.0` and everything works chethusk [13:26] and wait whaaaaaat Fabio Marreco [13:26] not sure of the consequences though chethusk [13:26] like you munged the `v4.0` part of the path to literally `XXXXv4.0`? Fabio Marreco [13:27] YEP, and ionide is all happy now I thought : "-Well, all the previous versions that I DONT want are in this folder, let me try to mess w/ it." ``` so the end result seems to be that we found an old version of FSharp.Core. The 15.8 changes don't bundle a version of FSharp.Core anymore, so we should think about how to find that.

title: A case of constant CPU usage
body:
So I started my investigation by noticing some nice RED power usage in Task Manager when I was doing something else than F#

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/131878/49545740-c2d57f80-f8de-11e8-8d1c-34b6534c3245.png)

Turns out it's an FSAC connected to Ionide in VSCode with my https://github.com/vbfox/MasterOfFoo project open... The CPU never goes down, always ~20%

Attaching a debugger show a constant barrage of background parsing :

```
fsautocomplete.exe Information: 0 : FCS: Unknown.TimeStampReferencedProject(g:\Code\MasterOfFoo\artifacts\BlackFox.MasterOfFoo\netstandard2.0\BlackFox.MasterOfFoo.dll).TryGetLogicalTimeStampForProject.CreateOneIncrementalBuilder (g:\Code\MasterOfFoo\src\BlackFox.MasterOfFoo\BlackFox.MasterOfFoo.fsproj)

fsautocomplete.exe Information: 0 : FCS: Unknown.CreateOneIncrementalBuilder (g:\Code\MasterOfFoo\src\BlackFox.MasterOfFoo\BlackFox.MasterOfFoo.fsproj)
fsautocomplete.exe Information: 0 : Reactor: 81,705.731 enqueue Unknown.GetBackgroundCheckResultsForFileInProject (g:\Code\MasterOfFoo\src\BlackFox.MasterOfFoo\obj\Debug\net45\BlackFox.MasterOfFoo.AssemblyInfo.fs), length 9
fsautocomplete.exe Information: 0 : Reactor: 81,705.738 enqueue Unknown.GetBackgroundCheckResultsForFileInProject (g:\Code\MasterOfFoo\artifacts\Version.fs), length 10
fsautocomplete.exe Information: 0 : Reactor: 81,705.768 enqueue Unknown.GetBackgroundCheckResultsForFileInProject (g:\Code\MasterOfFoo\src\BlackFox.MasterOfFoo\FormatSpecification.fs), length 11
fsautocomplete.exe Information: 0 : Reactor: 81,705.768 <-- Unknown.GetBackgroundCheckResultsForFileInProject, took 54.5193 ms
fsautocomplete.exe Information: 0 : Reactor: 81,705.792 pausing 10 milliseconds
fsautocomplete.exe Information: 0 : Reactor: 81,705.793 --> Unknown.GetBackgroundCheckResultsForFileInProject (g:\Code\MasterOfFoo\src\BlackFox.MasterOfFoo\PrintableElement.fs), remaining 11
fsautocomplete.exe Information: 0 : Reactor: 81,705.812 enqueue Unknown.GetBackgroundCheckResultsForFileInProject (g:\Code\MasterOfFoo\src\BlackFox.MasterOfFoo\PrintableElement.fs), length 11
fsautocomplete.exe Information: 0 : Reactor: 81,705.812 <-- Unknown.GetBackgroundCheckResultsForFileInProject, took 17.3633 ms
```

Attaching a profiler show a single working thread doing things with a long tail of calls in TypeChecker ([dotTrace file](https://mega.nz/#!rVJSlSQC!h_zzC50z4O95qVki7bT-4UfWwLOZNUNhis6JHdC02-A)):

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/131878/49546593-e8638880-f8e0-11e8-96e1-bfaab2ba0824.png)


![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/131878/49545920-573fe200-f8df-11e8-9ba7-853691a52009.png)

Ionide settings:

```json
{
  "FSharp.showProjectExplorerIn": "fsharp",
  "FSharp.showCodeOutlineIn": "fsharp",
  "FSharp.codeOutline": false,
  "FSharp.minimizeBackgroundParsing": true,

}
```

I didn't have language service requests debugging enabled so I'm not 100% sure that ionide wasn't the cause of the calls (But that would be surprising) enabling them live only give the websocket logs that looked like a repetition of the following lines :

```
[23:22:38 DEBUG] WebSocket message: '{"Kind":"errors","Data":{"File":"g:\\Code\\MasterOfFoo\\src\\BlackFox.MasterOfFoo\\FormatSpecification.fs","Errors":[]}}'

[23:22:38 DEBUG] WebSocket message: '{"Kind":"errors","Data":{"File":"g:\\Code\\MasterOfFoo\\src\\BlackFox.MasterOfFoo\\Core.FormatOptions.fs","Errors":[]}}'

[23:22:38 DEBUG] WebSocket message: '{"Kind":"errors","Data":{"File":"g:\\Code\\MasterOfFoo\\src\\BlackFox.MasterOfFoo\\Core.FormatToString.fs","Errors":[]}}'

title: New maintainers needed
body:
Hi,

I think it's pretty obvious that FSAC needs some more maintainers/people directly involved in project. Due to multiple reasons some of the people that's been involved with the project for long time are not really contributing or helping any more (and I'm not trying to blame anyone here, life happens).
 I became main contributor over last few years due to my work on Ionide and all the features/fixes we needed, but TBF I've never planned to become only maintainer on this project - I just don't have mental capability, nor time to handle yet another project as a serious maintainer of such "strategic" project. I'm still happy to code and contribute features (even big things like the proposed move to LSP - #320 ) but I just don't want to and I can't be main maintainer that handles stuff like releases, makes sure that CI passes, works on infrastructure etc (#36, #249, #284, #291, #307, #318).

So this issue, is kind of "call to action" - if anyone wants to help, if anyone wants to become maintainer of important project for F# community please let us know. We'd love your help.

CC: @rneatherway @forki @kjnilsson @enricosada @dsyme

title: Many GB per second are allocated after using FsAutoComplete solution for a while
body:
Actions:

* Build the project
* Open VSCode
* Open some files
* Write a few new functions that use FCS types

GC rollup:

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/6309070/51008372-54e52f00-1500-11e9-84ab-fa3cab351399.png)

53% of these allocations come from calling the SimplfyNameDiagnosticAnalyzer:

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/6309070/51008416-88c05480-1500-11e9-9e17-32dc49aba0f6.png)

Since the .NET GC is very good and the FSAC process is 64-bit this ends up not impacting performance after ~5 minutes of work, but I can see how a full day's worth of usage would become problematic.

title: support for .NET Template Engine (`dotnet new`)
body:
Allow FsAutocomplete to support .NET Template Engine ( https://github.com/dotnet/templating )
.NET Template Engine specify the format for .NET templates used by `dotnet new` and packaged as nupkg files (more info in https://github.com/dotnet/templating/wiki ).
The integration should allow FsAutocomplete client at least to:

- list all installed templates
- gather info about a template (options/parameters) so client can create UI
- create a new project, invoking `dotnet new` with parameters
- discover online templates (from a nuget feed like https://www.nuget.org/ ) and install it (invoking `dotnet new)

Some actions can be done invoking `dotnet new`, others may need use the .NET Template Engine library

title: upgrade to FCS 28
Consider using fsprojects/fsharp-language-server as a starting point for LSP
body:
I recently read on @Krzysztof-Cieslak twitter that he'll be working full-time on contract with MS for the next few months, and that one of the major focuses will be finishing the LSP implementation of FSAC. Congrats! 

For one moment, let me try to convince you (@Krzysztof-Cieslak) to use https://github.com/fsprojects/fsharp-language-server as a starting point for your efforts:
- Because of FSAC's history as a separate protocol, it contains a considerable amount of incidental complexity that isn't necessary in an LSP implementation. 
- In contrast, fsharp-language-server is a "clean slate" implementation of LSP. Under-the-hood, it uses FSharp.Compiler.Service, just like FSAC.
- If you migrate FSAC to the LSP, you will have to support both LSP and the legacy FSAC protocol for some time, which will make the job harder.
- On the other hand, if you "adopt" fsharp-language-server and port features from FSAC to it, you can make a clean break with the past.

I would be very happy to make @Krzysztof-Cieslak , @enricosada and any other maintainers of this repo admins on https://github.com/fsprojects/fsharp-language-server. I think you could very quickly port the features of FSAC that aren't in fsharp-language-server, such as the "type hints" that appear to the right of function definitions.

If I have failed to convince you of this proposal, there are several notable features of fsharp-language-server that it would still be worth trying to replicate in FSAC:

1. It builds as a standalone executable, see [here](https://github.com/fsprojects/fsharp-language-server/blob/master/scripts/build.sh#L11). This means it has no dependencies on system-installed `dotnet`, which eliminates a large category of errors.
2. It's significantly faster than FSAC. This is most easily observed in large projects---open something big, like FSharp.Compiler.Service, and click around and type to see how long autocomplete takes. The reason for this is that FSharp.Compiler.Service is extremely sensitive to the exact order-of-operations you send to it, because of the way its internal caches work. By fiddling with seemingly insignificant details of how I interacted with FSCS, I was able to obtain huge speedups. I don't recall all the details, but you should be able to get FSAC to be just as fast by fiddling with the order of operations you send to FSCS, and you can use fsharp-language-server as a reference of what speed is possible.
3. It contains some reusable e2e tests of [cracking difficult projects](https://github.com/fsprojects/fsharp-language-server/blob/master/tests/ProjectCracker.Tests/ProjectCrackerTests.fs) and tests of[ LSP features](https://github.com/fsprojects/fsharp-language-server/blob/master/tests/FSharpLanguageServer.Tests/ServerTests.fs). Because the LSP tests are written against the LSP protocol rather than any particular implementation, you could re-use them without too much difficulty.

On the other hand, if I have succeed in convincing you to try to "adopt" fsharp-language-server and port the missing functionality from FSAC, just let me know who to add as maintainers 😄 

@cartermp FYI

title: Add fsdn integration
body:
The website https://fsdn.azurewebsites.net/ allow to query for the signature of a function, and return the list of matching functions.

For example if i search for 

```
('a -> 'b) -> 'a list -> 'b list
```

i get as result 

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/147243/55330304-f9886480-5490-11e9-8f5a-e7aca9022883.png)

who contains `List.map` for example.

The query does a GET request (like [this](https://fsdn.azurewebsites.net/api/search?query=(%27a+-%3E+%27b)+-%3E+%27a+list+-%3E+%27b+list&exclusion=Argu%2BExtCore%2BFake%2BFParsec%2BFSharp.Collections.ParallelSeq%2BFSharp.Compiler.Service%2BFSharp.Control.AsyncSeq%2BFSharp.Control.Reactive%2BFSharp.Data%2BFSharp.ViewModule.Core%2BFsPickler%2BFsUnit%2BNewtonsoft.Json%2BSuave%2BSystem.Reactive&respect_name_difference=enabled&greedy_matching=disabled&ignore_parameter_style=enabled&ignore_case=enabled&substring=enabled&swap_order=enabled&complement=enabled&single_letter_as_variable=enabled&language=fsharp&limit=500) ) and return a json with the list of results

the results json contains the info of each functions, like the name:

```json
{"name":{"id":"map<'T, 'U>","class_name":"List","namespace":"Microsoft.FSharp.Collections.List"} }
```

The issue goal is to add a new command to FSAC to query fsdn website.


## Implementation

the new commands `fsdn` has one input:

- the query, string type. For example `('a -> 'b) -> 'a list -> 'b list` 

and one output:

- the list of result, with the full name of the function (namespace + class + method). string list. Like `[ "List.map<'T, 'U>" ]`


When the command is executed, it will do a GET request to the https://fsdn.azurewebsites.net/api/search url passing the arguments, and parse the json result.

title: fsautocomplete .net core doesnt run with just .net core 3 runtime installed
body:
fsautocomplete require a .net core runtime v2 installed.

Otherwise runnning `dotnet fsautocomplete.dll` results in:

```
It was not possible to find any compatible framework version
The specified framework 'Microsoft.NETCore.App', version '2.1.0' was not found.
  - Check application dependencies and target a framework version installed at:
      C:\dotnetcli\dotnet-sdk-3.0.100-preview3-010431-win-x64\
  - The .NET Core Runtime and SDK can be installed from:
      https://aka.ms/dotnet-download
  - The following versions are installed:
      3.0.0-preview3-27503-5 at [C:\dotnetcli\dotnet-sdk-3.0.100-preview3-010431-win-x64\shared\Microsoft.NETCore.App]
  - Installing .NET Core prerequisites might help resolve this problem:
      https://go.microsoft.com/fwlink/?linkid=798306
```

That's expected by .NET Core apps, because was compiled targeting 2.1 runtime, so by default as FDD will require `>= 2.1 < 3` (minor and path version upgrade allowed)

A workaround can be setting the runtime option `"rollForwardOnNoCandidateFx": 2` ( more info in  https://natemcmaster.com/blog/2019/01/09/netcore-primitives-3/#automatically-run-on-higher-major-or-minor-versions ) so when the application is executed, it will also allow to use runtime `>= 2.1`

title: Move KeywordList in FCS
body:
Move [src/FsAutoComplete.Core/KeywordList.fs](https://github.com/fsharp/FsAutoComplete/blob/f19b23d41b3e7fffe3d7ba947c27ff5ce15f1dc5/src/FsAutoComplete.Core/KeywordList.fs#L3-L13) data in FCS

- `KeywordList.modifiers`
- `KeywordList.keywordDescriptions`

The data contains the description of keywords and modifiers

```fsharp
module internal KeywordList =
    let private modifiers =
        Map.ofList [
            "abstract",  """Indicates a method that either has no implementation in the type in which it is declared or that is virtual and has a default implementation."""
             ...
        ]

    let private keywordDescriptions =
        Map.ofList [
            "and",  """Used in mutually recursive bindings, in property declarations, and with multiple constraints on generic parameters."""
            ...
```

title: Include 'runtimes' folder in netcore release archive
body:
After downloading the netcore release archive and trying to run it on OSX, `dotnet` complains with
```
Error:
  An assembly specified in the application dependencies manifest (fsautocomplete.deps.json) was not found:
    package: 'System.Data.SqlClient', version: '4.6.0'
    path: 'runtimes/unix/lib/netcoreapp2.1/System.Data.SqlClient.dll'
```
However, the version compiled from source runs just fine because of the presence of the assembly.

```

I currently don't have a repro for this.

title: Error loading project with custom `obj` and `bin`dir
body:
Given a project with custom intermediate path (the `obj`) and output path (the `bin`), the project fails to load

## How to repro

Create a new F# library, with a `Directory.Build.Props` like

```xml
<Project>
  <PropertyGroup>
		<BaseOutputPath>custom/bin/folder</BaseOutputPath>
		<BaseIntermediateOutputPath>custom/obj/folder</BaseIntermediateOutputPath>
	</PropertyGroup>
</Project>
```

## Expected

Project load correctly

## Actual

```json
Result: {
    "content": "{\"Kind\":\"error\",\"Data\":{\"Code\":101,\"Message\":\"Project file 'e:\\\\temp\\\\libredir\\\\libredir.fsproj' parsing failed\",\"AdditionalData\":{\"Project\":\"e:\\\\temp\\\\libredir\\\\libredir.fsproj\"}}}"
}
```

title: Improve output of `fsharp/documentation`

type.name: Enhancement
type.description: A request, idea, or new functionality

body: Right now, the output of `fsharp/documentation` is very ionide specific. For example, consider: ```json { "XmlKey": "M:Microsoft.FSharp.Collections.SeqModule.TryFind``1(Microsoft.FSharp.Core.FSharpFunc{``0,System.Boolean},System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{``0})", "Constructors": [], "Fields": [], "Functions": [], "Interfaces": [], "Attributes": [], "Types": [], "Signature": "val tryFind: \n predicate: 'T -> bool ->\n source : seq<'T> \n -> Option<'T>", "Comment": "**Description**\n\nReturns the first element for which the given function returns True.\n Return None if no such element exists.\n\n**Parameters**\n\n* `predicate`: A function that evaluates to a Boolean when given an item in the sequence.\n* `source`: The input sequence.\n\n**Returns**\n\nThe found element or None.\n\n**Exceptions**\n\n* `System.ArgumentNullException`: Thrown when the input sequence is null.\n\n**Generic parameters**\n\n* `'T` is `string`", "Footer": "Full name: Microsoft.FSharp.Collections.Seq.tryFind\nDeclaring Entity: Seq\nAssembly: FSharp.Core" } ``` This has a mix of markdown and html in it, as well as ionide specific commands, which makes it rather difficult to consume for other tools. I propose that we make the output a bit more generic by doing something along the lines of ```json { "XmlKey": "M:Microsoft.FSharp.Collections.SeqModule.TryFind``1(Microsoft.FSharp.Core.FSharpFunc{``0,System.Boolean},System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{``0})", "Constructors": [], "Fields": [], "Functions": [], "Interfaces": [], "Attributes": [], "Types": [], "Signature": { "Text" : "val tryFind: \n predicate: 'T -> bool ->\n source : seq<'T> -> \n Option<'T>", "Links": [{"Span" : { "Start" : , "End" : }, "Link" : "?%5B%7B%20%22XmlDocSig%22%3A%20%22T%3AMicrosoft.FSharp.Collections.SeqModule%22%2C%20%22AssemblyName%22%3A%20%22FSharp.Core%22%7D%5D"}, ...], "Comment" : { "Description" : "Returns the first element ...", "Parameters" : ..., ... } } ```

title: More URI woes
body:
After the last set of changes, I broke windows. I've replicated the failing scenario in a test case.

Windows urls are coming in like this: ` "uri": "file:///d%3A/code/Saturn/src/Saturn/Utils.fs",`, which when put through the `GetFilePath` extension method we have results in a file path of `/d:/code/Saturn/src/Saturn/Utils.fs`.

Shoot me now.

Now I propose that we keep the localpath-fetching logic, with one additional check:
* IF the localpath starts with a `/` 
  AND the next path segment contains one or more alphabetical letters
  THEN remove that leading slash

This seems really dumb and I hate everything about it.

title: Ability to customize Fantomas code formatting options
body:
The default settings are being used at the moment. We should consider adding the ability to configure the settings used.

Ideally this would be done in a way that can be overridden per repository.

Some comments from #editor_support slack thread that led to this isssue:

"It would be cool if Fantomas had some kind of settings.json files that we could load instead of adding all options to the Ionide's settings"

"yeah that would be great, then you could also run it in your build as an enforcement step and keep it consistent."

title: Allow customization/auto-detection of dotnet SDK root
body:
The default SDK roots don't work for everyone, so we should let that value be set by the user/discovered from runtime context before defaulting to our hard-coded value.

The algorithm should be something like:

* user-set config value (dotnetSDKRoot, maybe?)
* environment variable (DOTNET_ROOT is standard here)
* one of the following:
  * invoking `dotnet --info` and parsing out the `Base Path` line, or
  * invoking `which dotnet` (or windows equivalent) to find the location of the `dotnet` binary and using the parent directory of that binary
* the default value we have hard-coded

Most of this can go in Environment.fs I believe, but the hard part for me is deciding which of this should be in Ionide vs FSAC.  Because of the vagaries of the dotnet SDK, if you run `dotnet --info` from FSAC's path vs from the root of your project (which is what Ionide) would likely do) you could end up with different values for the Base Path. This is due to different PATH setups in different directories, etc.

title: Implement smart code folding
body: Per https://github.com/ionide/ionide-vscode-fsharp/issues/1226#issuecomment-539604434 we should implement the [`textDocument/foldingRange`](https://microsoft.github.io/language-server-protocol/specifications/specification-3-14/#textDocument_foldingRange) command to get smart fold ranges for the editor.

title: Discussion/Feature Request: expand the range of `textDocument/codeAction`

type.name: Enhancement
type.description: A request, idea, or new functionality

body:
First: this is an amazing project, and I thank y'all for it. _Especially_ LSP support; that has made my development life worlds better :D 

I want to raise a point of ergonomics; if this has already been discussed somewhere I'm just not finding, I do apologize! I tried to be thorough in my search of history, but there's... a lot of history. 

It goes like this. Say I've got this type:
```fsharp
type SomeRecord = 
    {Name: string
     Quality: string
     Quantity: int}
```
Great, now I go to instantiate it: 
```fsharp
let aRecord = 
    {Name = "some name"
    }
```
At this point, a code action becomes available (if enabled); I can stub out the remaining fields on this struct. The thing is, this code action is only available at a single point: between the opening curly brace and the first letter of the first field name. I'll use `|` for point:

```fsharp
let aRecord = 
    {|Name = "some name"
    }
```
This is a little awkward. It's hard to notice, and one must stop filling out record fields and jump back to a precise point at the start of the record, _then_ descend back in to replace the fields with real values.  

I'd like to propose -- or at least, discuss -- a possible ergonomic enhancement to this. Would it be possible to mark the entire form with the code action? Fire the "stub out record" even from anywhere in the braces? 

Likewise, the README says there's a code action for implementing FSLint suggestions, but I cannot figure out how to fire it. It strikes me that those rather apply to the whole document, from a code-action perspective. Maybe not? 

In any case. Thanks for the wonderful project, the time, and the consideration.

title: Go-to-definition decompiler doesn't work for the F# references.
body:
For some reason FCS reports `FSharpFindDeclResult.DeclFound` for symbols declared in external F# libraries - we then handle it at normal go-to and attempt to return the result to the client, which ends up in error as such file probably doesn't exist on user machine. Example (note it reports path from @baronfel machine, when I try to do go-to-def on my machine):

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/5427083/67105357-eb016080-f1c8-11e9-97de-2888b23af439.png)

It seems it's FCS problem (coming from here? - https://github.com/fsharp/FSharp.Compiler.Service/blob/1471a682b5fd3de170d117e2d55adfad7a617d16/src/fsharp/service/FSharpCheckerResults.fs#L1216-L1221) but we should be able to workaround it by checking if returned file exists, and if not just decompile as with C# references

title: [LSP] Need way of allowing users to set the `Debug.verbose` property
body: We need a way to set the `Debug.verbose` property so that users can get the inner, actual logs (especially from the FSX script context or inner failures of typechecking).  Right now helping users figure out _why_ things are failing is a crapshoot.

title: Windows - cross-file types not updated
body: I'm not seeing updated types reflected across files on Windows 10, `:%s/\r\n/\r/` on `obj\Debug\<target>\<project>.AssemblyInfo.fs` seems to get it working again.

title: Add a way to pass in FSI additional options to use when typechecking
body:
Right now we assume `--langversion:preview` when typechecking scripts, because this in no way impacts things if a user doesn't use any preview features. This may not always be the case in the future, depending on which features end up behind the preview langversion flag.

To keep FSI and the typechecker in sync, we should provide a way for any user-configured FSI options (for example the `"FSharp.fsiExtraParameters"` parameter in ionide) to be passed in to the FSAC instance, and be added onto the options used for typechecking scripts.  The current settings should be mutable, and probably stored at the same level/in the same way as the current SDK paths.

title: Inconsistent Type Signatures on Hover
body:
In VsCode, there are 3 places that type signature occur:
1. info panel (down the bottom)
2. comment style (if enabled)
3. dialog box on hover

Currently, number 3 is broken:
```
let test = "a", [|1|], [| 1,2 |]
```

With 1 and 2, this is correctly displayed as `string * int[] * (int * int) []`, however, on hover (3) it shows `val test : string * int array * int * int array`, which is incorrect. Example: 
![screenshot](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/50384876/68552746-9c18b500-046e-11ea-9cfa-1ea1f370e9b6.png)

title: Inconsistent Type Signatures on Hover #2
body:
Following on from #498 , here is another interesting one:
```
let edges = [ struct(1u,0); struct(2u,0) ]
``` 
Hover over edges here shows `list<uint32 * int>` as opposed to `list<struct(uint32*int)>`, the correct signature in the inline comments and also the info panel.

It leads to some issues as the easiest way to copy type signature in ionide+vscode is the hover dialog

</details>

<details>
<summary>The parse errors we get</summary>

```
[18:05:30 DBG] event: (textDocument/publishDiagnostics, { Uri =
       "file:///Users/chethusk/oss/FsAutoComplete/test/FsAutoComplete.Tests.Lsp/TestCases/Tooltips/Script.fsx"
  Diagnostics =
               [|{ Range = { Start = { Line = 0
                                       Character = 0 }
                             End = { Line = 0
                                     Character = 0 } }
                   Severity = Some Error
                   Code = Some "84"
                   Source = "F# Compiler"
                   Message =
                            "Assembly reference 'System.Runtime.Remoting.dll' was not found or is invalid"
                   RelatedInformation = Some [||]
                   Tags = None };
                 { Range = { Start = { Line = 0
                                       Character = 0 }
                             End = { Line = 0
                                     Character = 0 } }
                   Severity = Some Error
                   Code = Some "84"
                   Source = "F# Compiler"
                   Message =
                            "Assembly reference 'System.Runtime.Serialization.Formatters.Soap.dll' was not found or is invalid"
                   RelatedInformation = Some [||]
                   Tags = None };
                 { Range = { Start = { Line = 0
                                       Character = 0 }
                             End = { Line = 0
                                     Character = 0 } }
                   Severity = Some Error
                   Code = Some "84"
                   Source = "F# Compiler"
                   Message =
                            "Assembly reference 'System.Web.Services.dll' was not found or is invalid"
                   RelatedInformation = Some [||]
                   Tags = None };
                 { Range = { Start = { Line = 0
                                       Character = 0 }
                             End = { Line = 0
                                     Character = 0 } }
                   Severity = Some Error
                   Code = Some "84"
                   Source = "F# Compiler"
                   Message =
                            "Assembly reference 'System.Windows.Forms.dll' was not found or is invalid"
                   RelatedInformation = Some [||]
                   Tags = None }|] }) <LSPTests>
```

</details>

So the gist is that _something_ in the compiler/FSI typechecking is including those somehow?

We _should_ be able to filter out these erroneous references (they are cosmetic only) but after we do that short-term fix we should report upstream.  Also will need to validate this after updating to FCS 33.

title: Deprecating Full Framework version of FSAC
body:
I think we all agree that in terms of tooling host .Net Core is better, more stable target. And that .Net Core is a future of .Net. So my proposal would be to remove multi-targeting from FSAC, and target just .Net Core as runtime platform. 

Keep in mind it doesn't mean dropping support for Full Framework projects, it just means you need .Net SDK to run FSAC.

title: Installation of FSAC with dotnet tool
body:
Hello,

FSAC is installed by manually or automatically by the scripts of each plugin. I propose a new installation with `dotnet tool`. This method is used by some official utilities such as `dotnet-try`, and a few F# projects. FAKE is one example.

## Pros:
- Each plugin can call the API without a specific script. It is `dotnet tool install fsautocomplete`.
- Quite simple to install FSAC alone. 

## Cons:
- The project should be uploaded on NuGet.
- They cannot use nightly build with this method.

Second pros., is important for using FSAC with emacs and vim plugins such as vim-lsp, ALE and eglot. Do you have any plan to distribute binary build with `dotnet tool`?

Thanks.

title: Memory leak
body:
Making an issue here at the request of @Krzysztof-Cieslak .  When using Ionide on a large solution (60+ projects), there is a memory leak.

Details originally in this issue: https://github.com/dotnet/diagnostics/issues/669

The leaky process from that original issue was

```
nat               7949  31.2  4.4 40483832 735964   ??  R     9:02AM 121:41.58 dotnet /Users/nat/.vscode/extensions/ionide.ionide-fsharp-4.3.2/bin_netcore/fsautocomplete.backgroundservices.dll 7911
```

which, while I was not actively using Ionide (it was just open in the background), eventually consumed 21GB of memory before I closed it.

I haven't been able to get it down to a minimal repro, but I'll report back if I do.

title: Discarded this and member private shows error
body:
Discarded this and member private shows error but does compile (potentially other forms don't take into account discard either)

```
    member _.Foo() = () // no squiggle
    member private _.Foo() = () // squiggle
```

title: ERROR: unrecognized argument: "--mode"

title: Expose diagnostics endpoint to view versions of dependencies
body:
It would be nice if there was some sort of endpoint that returns the versions of all the tools used by FSAutocomplete.

Fantomas for example exposes the version and this could be useful when logging issues.
Or show that version in the about section of Ionide or diagnostics dump.

//cc @baronfel

title: Improve logging subsystem
body:
We should replace the current printf-based logger with a structured logger.

Ideally any logger we'd use would:

* provide categories as a first-class feature
* support structured logging
* support logging to multiple outputs (ie. stdout + file)
* support good formatting of F# values
* be configurable in terms of which categories are logged and which log levels are written

To fulfill all of those requirements I suggest using [Serilog](https://github.com/serilog/serilog), through the [fsliblog](https://github.com/TheAngryByrd/FsLibLog) facade with the [F# support](https://github.com/destructurama/fsharp/) library.

As far as configuration, I'd add something like the following configuration to the current F# config (obsoleteing some of the existing settings):

```json
{
  "logging": {
	"level": "<log level string>",
    "include_categories": ["<list of categories to log at the above log level _only_ >"] | { "<category>": "<log level>" },  // list of categories would need to be documented somewhere
    "exclude_categories": ["<list of categories to never log>"],
    "logOutputs": [ "stdout", "file" ]
}
```

title: Autocomplete issue
Provide an AST model of a file or selection

type.name: Enhancement
type.description: A request, idea, or new functionality

body:
It would be helpful for tool developers (or those developing an analyzer!) to have an easy way to view the AST of the current file/the current selection in some way. I was thinking endpoints like:

```
type AstMode = | Text // later on add a 'json' or 'graph' mode or something for better views
type GetDocumentAstParams = 
  { Document: TextDocumentIdentifier
    AstMode: AstMode }

let getAstForTextDocument: GetDocumentAstParams -> LspResult<some_kind_of_text>

type GetSelectionAstParams = 
  { Document: TextDocumentIdentifier
    Range: <whatever the LSP range type is>
    AstMode: AstMode }

let getAstForSelection: GetSelectionAstParams -> LspResult<some_kind_of_text>
```

And then clients could specify the format they wanted and render accordingly.  First pass could just be the `sprintf "%A"` representation of the AST, but later iterations could embed more of the functionality found in https://jindraivanek.gitlab.io/ast-viewer/ into dedicated views if there was demand.

This is a bit of a selfish feature, because having this would make writing analyzers easier for me specifically :)

title: [FAKE Support] Broken after latest FCS Update
body:
Newer FCS versions have introduced new optional parameters to the FSharpChecker.Create call, and the Fake Support uses YaafFSharpScripting which hasn't yet been updated. This means we get the following exception at runtime:

```
[10:00:04 ERR] [Opts] Error in FAKE script support
System.TypeInitializationException: The type initializer for '<StartupCode$Fake-Runtime>.$YaafFSharpScripting' threw an exception. --->
System.MissingMethodException: Method not found: 'FSharp.Compiler.SourceCodeServices.FSharpChecker FSharp.Compiler.SourceCodeServices.FSharpChecker.Create(Microsoft.FSharp.Core.FSharpOption`1<Int32>, Microsoft.FSharp.Core.FSharpOption`1<Boolean>, Microsoft.FSharp.Core.FSharpOption`1<Boolean>, Microsoft.FSharp.Core.FSharpOption`1<Resolver>, Microsoft.FSharp.Core.FSharpOption`1<Microsoft.FSharp.Core.FSharpFunc`2<System.Tuple`2<System.String,System.DateTime>,Microsoft.FSharp.Core.FSharpOption`1<System.Tuple`3<System.Object,IntPtr,Int32>>>>, Microsoft.FSharp.Core.FSharpOption`1<Boolean>)'.
```

We need to update YaafFSharpScripting and then update FSAC to make FAKE Support work again.
